task: Write Terraform HCL for a production-grade AWS deployment with VPC, ECS Fargate, RDS, and ALB across dev/staging/prod environments using workspaces and modules, with least-privilege IAM

criteria.description: IAM policies use aws_iam_policy_document data sources with layered composition rather than inline JSON strings
criteria.pass_condition: All IAM policies constructed using aws_iam_policy_document with source_policy_documents or override_policy_documents for modular composition, no hardcoded JSON policy strings

sub_attributes.description: Uses aws_iam_policy_document data sources instead of inline JSON
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if all policies use data sources, 0.7 if majority use data sources, 0.3 if mixed approach, 0.0 if inline JSON

sub_attributes.description: Implements modular policy composition with source_policy_documents
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if uses layered composition patterns, 0.6 if basic data source structure, 0.0 if monolithic policies

sub_attributes.description: Policies are scoped to specific resources with condition blocks
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if resource-specific with conditions, 0.7 if resource-specific, 0.3 if service-level, 0.0 if broad permissions

fail_examples: resource 'aws_iam_role_policy' 'ecs' { policy = jsonencode({ Version = '2012-10-17', Statement = [{ Effect = 'Allow', Action = '*', Resource = '*' }] }) }
criteria.research_basis: AWS Well-Architected Security Pillar recommends granular IAM policies with least-privilege access. HashiCorp Terraform AWS Provider documentation emphasizes aws_iam_policy_document for maintainable policy composition.

criteria.description: Implements VPC endpoints for AWS services to maintain private API communication
criteria.pass_condition: Includes interface VPC endpoints for at least ECS, ECR, S3, CloudWatch services used by the infrastructure, avoiding internet routing for AWS API calls

sub_attributes.description: VPC endpoints for critical services (ECS, ECR, S3, CloudWatch)
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if 4+ service endpoints, 0.7 if 3 endpoints, 0.4 if 2 endpoints, 0.0 if 0-1 endpoints

sub_attributes.description: VPC endpoints properly secured with security groups and policies
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if security groups + policy restrictions, 0.6 if security groups only, 0.0 if default configuration

sub_attributes.description: Private DNS enabled for seamless service integration
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if private_dns_enabled = true, 0.0 if disabled or missing

pass_examples: resource 'aws_vpc_endpoint' 'ecs' { vpc_id = aws_vpc.main.id service_name = 'com.amazonaws.${data.aws_region.current.name}.ecs' vpc_endpoint_type = 'Interface' security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.vpc_endpoints.id] private_dns_enabled = true }
fail_examples: No VPC endpoints defined, relying on internet gateway for AWS API calls
criteria.research_basis: CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark 5.4 recommends VPC endpoints to avoid internet routing for AWS service API calls. AWS Security Best Practices emphasize private API communication.

criteria.description: Avoids common Terraform anti-patterns that create maintenance and reliability issues
criteria.pass_condition: No hardcoded availability zones (uses data sources for AZ discovery), module source includes version constraints or specific git refs, no workspace-specific conditional logic within shared module code, and no embedded provider configurations in modules
criteria.research_basis: HashiCorp Terraform Best Practices Guide prohibits hardcoded infrastructure identifiers. Terraform Module Standards require version pinning for production stability.

criteria.description: ECS Fargate configuration follows security best practices with proper task roles and network isolation
criteria.pass_condition: ECS tasks use dedicated task roles, run in private subnets, implement read-only root filesystem, and use specific image tags (not :latest)

sub_attributes.description: Dedicated IAM task roles for each service with least-privilege permissions
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if unique task roles per service, 0.6 if shared role with scoped permissions, 0.0 if overly broad or missing task roles

sub_attributes.description: Tasks run in private subnets with proper security group restrictions
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if private subnets + restrictive security groups, 0.5 if private subnets only, 0.0 if public subnets

sub_attributes.description: Read-only root filesystem and specific image tags (not :latest)
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if read-only filesystem + specific tags, 0.6 if one of two, 0.0 if neither

sub_attributes.description: Sensitive data handled via Secrets Manager or Parameter Store
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if uses AWS secrets services with proper IAM, 0.4 if environment variables with encryption, 0.0 if plain text secrets

pass_examples: task_role_arn = aws_iam_role.ecs_task_role.arn, readonlyRootFilesystem: true, image: 'myapp:v1.2.3', subnets = aws_subnet.private[*].id
fail_examples: image: 'myapp:latest', subnets = aws_subnet.public[*].id, no task role specified
criteria.research_basis: CIS Docker Benchmark 5.12 requires read-only root filesystem. AWS ECS Security Best Practices mandate task roles and private networking for production workloads.

criteria.description: ALB implements proper security controls with SSL/TLS configuration and network access restrictions
criteria.pass_condition: ALB configured with HTTPS listeners, proper SSL security policy, certificate management via ACM, security groups restricting access to necessary ports/sources, and proper health check configuration

sub_attributes.description: Proper CloudFront-ALB integration with origin restrictions
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if CloudFront with custom origin headers, 0.6 if CloudFront without headers, 0.3 if basic CloudFront, 0.0 if no CloudFront

sub_attributes.description: Security groups restrict traffic to CloudFront or VPC origins only
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if restricted to CloudFront IPs or VPC, 0.5 if limited port access, 0.0 if open to internet

sub_attributes.description: Proper SSL/TLS configuration with security policy and certificate management
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if TLS 1.2+ policy + ACM certificates, 0.6 if proper certificates only, 0.0 if weak SSL configuration

pass_examples: aws_lb.main { internal = false } with security groups allowing only CloudFront prefix lists, or internal = true with CloudFront VPC origins
fail_examples: ALB open to 0.0.0.0/0 without CloudFront restrictions or origin controls
criteria.research_basis: AWS Well-Architected Security Pillar recommends origin access control for public-facing applications. CloudFront security documentation emphasizes origin header validation.

criteria.description: RDS configuration implements comprehensive security controls including encryption, network isolation, and backup security
criteria.pass_condition: RDS in private subnets, encryption at rest and in transit, automated backups with encryption, parameter groups with security settings, no public access

sub_attributes.description: Encryption at rest and in transit with KMS key management
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if custom KMS keys for encryption at rest + SSL enforcement, 0.7 if default encryption + SSL, 0.3 if encryption only, 0.0 if no encryption

sub_attributes.description: Private subnets, security groups, and no public accessibility
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if private subnets + restrictive SG + publicly_accessible = false, 0.6 if two of three, 0.0 if publicly accessible

sub_attributes.description: Encrypted automated backups with appropriate retention and point-in-time recovery
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if encrypted backups + 7+ day retention + PITR, 0.6 if encrypted backups only, 0.0 if unencrypted or minimal retention

sub_attributes.description: Custom parameter group with security-focused settings
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if custom parameter group with security settings, 0.5 if custom group with basic settings, 0.0 if default parameter group

pass_examples: storage_encrypted = true, kms_key_id = aws_kms_key.rds.arn, ssl_enforcement_enabled = true, publicly_accessible = false, db_subnet_group_name in private subnets
fail_examples: publicly_accessible = true, storage_encrypted = false, using default parameter group
criteria.research_basis: CIS AWS RDS Benchmark requires encryption at rest and in transit, private network placement, and custom parameter groups for production databases.

criteria.description: Terraform state backend properly secured with encryption, locking, and access controls
criteria.pass_condition: S3 backend with server-side encryption, DynamoDB locking, versioning enabled, bucket policy restricting access, no state stored locally
pass_examples: terraform { backend 's3' { bucket = 'terraform-state', encrypt = true, dynamodb_table = 'terraform-locks', versioning = true } }
fail_examples: Local backend, S3 backend without encryption, missing DynamoDB table for locking
criteria.research_basis: HashiCorp Terraform Enterprise documentation mandates encrypted remote state with locking for production environments. AWS security best practices require encrypted S3 storage.

criteria.description: Module structure follows Terraform best practices with proper separation of concerns and reusability
criteria.pass_condition: Creates separate modules for networking (VPC, subnets, routing), compute (ECS cluster, services, task definitions), and data (RDS, parameter groups); each module has typed variables with validation rules and structured outputs with descriptions; no cross-layer resource dependencies within modules

sub_attributes.description: Logical separation between networking, compute, and data modules
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if clear module separation (vpc, ecs, rds modules), 0.6 if some separation, 0.0 if monolithic structure

sub_attributes.description: Input variables include validation rules and type constraints
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if validation blocks + type constraints, 0.6 if type constraints only, 0.0 if basic string variables

sub_attributes.description: Well-designed outputs enabling module composition and integration
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if structured outputs with descriptions, 0.5 if basic outputs, 0.0 if minimal or missing outputs

sub_attributes.description: Modules properly parameterized to work across dev/staging/prod without modification
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if fully parameterized for multi-env, 0.6 if mostly parameterized, 0.0 if environment-specific logic

pass_examples: modules/vpc/, modules/ecs/, modules/rds/ with variable validation blocks and comprehensive outputs
fail_examples: Single main.tf file with all resources, variables without type constraints or validation
criteria.research_basis: HashiCorp Terraform Module Standards recommend separation of concerns and proper variable validation for maintainable infrastructure code.

criteria.description: Terraform workspace strategy properly configured for dev/staging/prod with environment-specific variables and consistent resource naming
criteria.pass_condition: Uses terraform.tfvars files or workspace-specific variable files, consistent naming conventions with environment prefixes, no workspace-conditional logic in modules

sub_attributes.description: Environment-specific variables managed through tfvars files or similar
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if dedicated tfvars per environment, 0.6 if workspace variables, 0.2 if hardcoded differences, 0.0 if no environment distinction

sub_attributes.description: Consistent resource naming with environment identification
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if systematic naming with env prefix/suffix, 0.6 if mostly consistent, 0.0 if ad-hoc naming

sub_attributes.description: Clean separation between environments without cross-environment dependencies
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if complete isolation with separate backends, 0.6 if logical separation, 0.0 if shared resources across environments

pass_examples: terraform/environments/dev.tfvars, terraform/environments/prod.tfvars with consistent resource naming patterns
fail_examples: Mixed environment configuration in single files, inconsistent naming across environments
criteria.research_basis: Terraform Cloud workspace documentation and HashiCorp best practices emphasize environment isolation and variable management for production deployments.

criteria.description: Implements comprehensive role-based authentication eliminating long-term credentials and users
criteria.pass_condition: All service-to-service authentication uses IAM roles with assume role patterns, ECS task roles for container authentication, no IAM users for automated systems

sub_attributes.description: Inter-service communication uses assume role patterns instead of embedded credentials
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if assume role policies for all cross-service access, 0.6 if mostly role-based, 0.2 if mixed approach, 0.0 if user-based authentication

sub_attributes.description: ECS tasks use task roles for AWS service authentication
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if task roles for all ECS services, 0.6 if most services have task roles, 0.0 if using instance roles or embedded credentials

sub_attributes.description: No IAM users or long-term access keys for automated systems
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if zero IAM users for automation, 0.5 if minimal IAM users with justification, 0.0 if automated systems use IAM users

sub_attributes.description: All authentication relies on temporary credentials with appropriate session duration
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if explicit session duration controls, 0.5 if default temporary credentials, 0.0 if long-term credentials present

pass_examples: ECS task definitions with task_role_arn, assume role policies for cross-service access, no aws_iam_user resources for automation
fail_examples: IAM users with programmatic access for ECS tasks, hardcoded access keys, missing task roles
criteria.research_basis: AWS Security Token Service documentation and IAM best practices mandate temporary credentials for all automated systems. CIS AWS Benchmark prohibits long-term access keys for applications.

criteria.description: Comprehensive logging and monitoring with security-focused configuration and proper log encryption
criteria.pass_condition: CloudWatch log groups with KMS encryption, VPC Flow Logs enabled, ALB access logs to S3 with encryption, ECS container logging configured with log drivers, and appropriate log retention policies set for all log groups

sub_attributes.description: All log streams encrypted with KMS keys
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if custom KMS keys for all log groups, 0.6 if default encryption, 0.0 if unencrypted logs

sub_attributes.description: VPC Flow Logs, ALB access logs, and application logs all configured
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if all three log types configured, 0.7 if two types, 0.3 if one type, 0.0 if minimal logging

sub_attributes.description: Appropriate retention periods for different log types (security vs application)
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if differentiated retention (security logs longer), 0.6 if consistent retention policies, 0.0 if default or no retention

sub_attributes.description: Logs configured for security analysis and alerting
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if structured JSON logging with security fields, 0.5 if basic structured logging, 0.0 if unstructured logs

pass_examples: aws_cloudwatch_log_group with kms_key_id, aws_flow_log for VPC, ALB access_logs enabled to encrypted S3
fail_examples: Default CloudWatch logging without encryption, missing VPC Flow Logs, no access log retention
criteria.research_basis: AWS CloudTrail and CloudWatch security documentation requires encrypted logging for production workloads. CIS AWS Benchmark mandates VPC Flow Logs and access logging for security monitoring.

criteria.description: Comprehensive and consistent resource tagging for cost allocation, security, and compliance tracking
criteria.pass_condition: All resources tagged with Environment, Owner, Project, CostCenter tags; sensitive resources tagged for compliance tracking; tags implemented via locals or modules for consistency
pass_examples: locals { common_tags = { Environment = var.environment, Owner = var.owner, Project = var.project_name, CostCenter = var.cost_center } }
fail_examples: Resources without tags, inconsistent tag naming across resources, missing required compliance tags
criteria.research_basis: AWS Tagging Best Practices require consistent tagging for cost allocation and compliance. Many compliance frameworks (SOC2, HIPAA) mandate resource classification through tagging.
